name: Apples and Buttons (Schnitz Un Knepp)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over and wash the dried apples. Cover with water and let soak overnight or for several hours.
2. In a large pot, cover the ham with cold water and bring to a boil. Boil for 3 hours until tender.
3. Add the soaked apples and their soaking liquid to the pot. Continue boiling for another hour.
4. Add brown sugar to the mixture.
5. Make dumplings by sifting together flour, salt, pepper, and baking powder. Stir in beaten egg, milk (enough to make a fairly moist, stiff batter), and melted butter until combined.
6. Drop spoonfuls of batter into the pot with the ham and apples. Cover tightly and cook for 15 minutes.
7. Serve hot on a large platter.
Source: Pennsylvania Dutch Cook Book - Fine Old Recipes, Culinary Arts Press, 1936.
